system.description: Throughout central Asia, wrestling is the ultimate sport; both peasants and sultans alike cheer on their favorites in the ring, and victors can become famous and wealthy. While it is a popular form of entertainment, Kurash wrestling can also be a lethal means of combat. Kurash is a battle of skill, speed, strength, and above all, endurance; matches have been known to last for days.

benefit.description:
If you have an opponent in a Grapple, you can physically hurl them through the air and onto the ground by making a Shove/Trip attack against them at an increased difficulty (-2). If you succeed, the opponent is on the ground 1d6 feet away, At a Loss, and cannot recover until spending a Major Action or Yield Advantage.

You can also use the Beat Maneuver unarmed, even against an armed opponent, without any problem, as you simply push the opponent’s body off- balance, or hit their hand or haft of their weapon.
benefit.name: Lift and Throw

finalSecret.description: Whenever you take damage, you can spend a Fortune Point to reduce the damage by 3 instead of by 2, or by 5 instead of by 4 against Pawns. After battle, you can spend a Fortune Point to recover 2 Lifeblood instead of 1. Your Maximum Lifeblood is also permanently increased by 1. Lastly, you get a Bonus Die on any roll to test long-term physical exertion, such as treading water, long distance running, etc.
finalSecret.name: Unyielding
